one of the weirdest things I've witnessed in my career is Michael Burry becoming an AI booster that also pretends to care about the economics. He actively (and nonsensically - Claude Cowork doesn't really work!) boosts Anthropic again and again and refuses to cover the actual economics side.

We had a new person come to the Kubernetes SIG Security meeting today and ask such great questions. What’s that mean? How does that work? Why is it this way? How does that get decided?

New contributors, don’t ever doubt yourselves. You bring such valuable perspective that makes open source better!

I caught up with Matt Klein, one of the famed developers behind the Envoy Proxy, and had a chat about AI and why the hype machine has gone too far. I think the industry is putting machines over people and the tradeoff leans too far in the rent seeker's favor.

Kubernetes Contributors,

We've updated the AI use policy. TL;DR you must disclose AI use in the PR description and interact with reviewers yourself. You must not co-author with AI or co-sign commits with AI or use the assisted-by or co-developed commit trailers.
